question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by submitting crafted balance stream files to the public cloud bucket such that the importer's scheduler, driven by `frequency` (which has `@NotNull` but no `@DurationMin` constraint), is configured to 0ms or 1ms, causing `AccountBalancesDownloader.download()` to spin in a tight loop and exhaust all JVM scheduler threads, halting balance ingestion for the entire network?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the fact that `Duration.ofSeconds(30)` default for `frequency` has no lower-bound validation, so that if an operator misconfigures `frequency=0s` (a valid `Duration` that passes `@NotNull`), `toMillis()` returns 0 and Spring's `@Scheduled` fires the `download()` method in a tight loop, consuming 100% CPU and preventing any other importer tasks from executing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based disk-exhaustion DoS by uploading an unbounded number of large balance stream files (up to `maxSize` = 50 MiB each) to the public cloud bucket, knowing that when `persistBytes=true` is configured, every downloaded balance file's raw bytes are persisted to the database, filling the database disk and halting all importer operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based filesystem DoS by flooding the public cloud bucket with maximum-size (50 MiB) balance stream files, knowing that when `writeFiles=true` is configured, each verified stream file is written to the local filesystem path under `importerProperties.getDataPath()`, exhausting available disk space and crashing the importer process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based filesystem DoS by flooding the cloud bucket with a large number of balance signature files, knowing that when `writeSignatures=true` is configured, all verified signature files are written to disk, and with no rate limiting or disk quota enforcement in `BalanceDownloaderProperties`, the filesystem fills up and the importer crashes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the `shouldDownload()` fallback logic in `AccountBalancesDownloader`—where even when `enabled=false`, the downloader still attempts to download if no balance file exists in the DB—by ensuring the DB is empty (e.g., via a fresh deployment) and flooding the bucket with malformed balance files, causing repeated failed download attempts that exhaust DB connection pool resources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the fact that `getStreamType()` always returns `StreamType.BALANCE` with no runtime guard, and by crafting balance stream files that contain record-stream-type content, causing type confusion in the parser pipeline that triggers repeated exceptions and retry loops, exhausting the retry thread pool and halting balance processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the shared `CommonDownloaderProperties` instance (injected via `common` field) to influence `batchSize=25`, and by placing exactly 25 maximally-sized (50 MiB) balance files per node in the bucket, causing each `downloadNextBatch()` invocation to allocate 25 × 50 MiB = 1.25 GiB of heap per batch, triggering OOM and crashing the importer JVM?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the SpEL expression `#{@balanceDownloaderProperties.getFrequency().toMillis()}` in the `@Scheduled` annotation—where if `frequency` is set to a `Duration` whose `toMillis()` overflows to a negative value (e.g., `Duration.ofNanos(Long.MAX_VALUE)`), Spring's scheduler may behave unexpectedly, causing immediate repeated invocations that exhaust CPU resources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based database DoS by crafting balance stream files that are exactly at the `maxSize` limit (50 MiB) and ensuring `persistBytes=true`, so that each processed file writes 50 MiB of raw bytes to the database, and with `batchSize=25` files per cycle, each batch writes up to 1.25 GiB to the DB, exhausting database storage and causing all importer DB operations to fail?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the missing lower-bound constraint on `frequency` to set it to `PT0.001S` (1 millisecond), causing the `@Scheduled` task to fire 1000 times per second, each invocation attempting to list and download from the cloud bucket, exhausting the `maxConcurrency=1000` HTTP connection pool and starving all other downloader tasks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the race condition in `AccountBalancesDownloader.shouldDownload()` where `accountBalanceFileExists` AtomicBoolean is checked before the DB query—by timing balance file uploads to the bucket such that the importer repeatedly re-enters the download loop before the AtomicBoolean is set, causing concurrent download attempts that exhaust DB connection pool resources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by uploading a continuous stream of balance files to the public bucket, knowing that with `writeFiles=true` and no file count or total size limit in `BalanceDownloaderProperties`, the importer writes every verified file to the local data path indefinitely, filling the filesystem and causing the importer process to crash with an IOException?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the fact that `frequency` accepts any non-null `Duration` including `Duration.ZERO`, and that `Duration.ZERO.toMillis()` returns 0, which when used in `fixedDelayString` causes Spring to schedule the task with 0ms delay, effectively creating a busy-wait loop that consumes all available CPU cycles and prevents the JVM GC from running, leading to OOM?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based memory DoS by crafting balance stream files that are maximally sized (50 MiB) and enabling `persistBytes=true`, so that the importer loads the entire file content into a byte array in memory before persisting, and with `batchSize=25` concurrent downloads, allocates 25 × 50 MiB = 1.25 GiB of heap simultaneously, triggering a JVM OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by uploading balance files with corrupted or oversized headers that pass the `maxSize` check but cause the `BalanceFileReader` to allocate unbounded internal buffers during parsing, exhausting JVM heap memory and crashing the importer, given that `BalanceDownloaderProperties` imposes no per-record or per-line size limit?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the `shouldDownload()` logic where `enabled=false` still triggers a DB query (`accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest()`) on every scheduler tick, and by setting `frequency` to a very small value, causing thousands of DB queries per second that exhaust the database connection pool and starve all other importer DB operations?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the lack of a `@DurationMin` constraint on `frequency` to configure it as a negative `Duration` (e.g., `Duration.ofSeconds(-1)`), causing `toMillis()` to return a negative value that, when passed to Spring's `fixedDelayString`, triggers an `IllegalArgumentException` at startup that prevents the entire importer application from starting, resulting in a complete DoS of balance and record ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by flooding the cloud bucket with a large number of balance signature files per consensus node, knowing that with `writeSignatures=true` and `batchSize=25`, each download cycle writes up to 25 × N_nodes signature files to disk, and with no total disk usage cap in `BalanceDownloaderProperties`, the filesystem fills up and crashes the importer?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the shared `CommonDownloaderProperties.maxSize=50MiB` limit—which is the only size guard—by crafting balance files that are exactly 50 MiB and contain deeply nested or repetitive data structures that cause the parser to expand them into much larger in-memory object graphs, exhausting JVM heap and triggering OOM?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the fact that `frequency` is read via SpEL at scheduler initialization time and is not re-evaluated dynamically, so that if an attacker can influence the Spring Environment (e.g., via a writable config source) to set `frequency=1ms` before startup, the scheduler permanently runs at 1ms intervals, causing continuous CPU and I/O exhaustion with no way to recover without a restart?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by uploading balance files containing maximally large account balance entries (millions of accounts) to the public bucket, knowing that with `persistBytes=true`, the raw bytes of each file are stored in the DB, and with `parser.balance.batchSize=200000` and `parser.balance.batch.maxItems=1000000`, the parser accumulates up to 1 million balance entries in memory before flushing, causing heap exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the interaction between `BalanceDownloaderProperties.enabled=false` and `HistoricalBalanceProperties.enabled=true`—where the `init()` method in `HistoricalBalanceProperties` throws `IllegalArgumentException` only when both are true—by crafting a scenario where both are simultaneously enabled via environment variable injection, crashing the Spring context at startup and preventing the importer from starting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network-based DoS by exploiting the fact that `frequency` has no `@DurationMax` constraint, allowing it to be set to `Duration.ofDays(Long.MAX_VALUE / 86400)`, causing `toMillis()` to overflow to a negative or zero value that makes the scheduler fire immediately and continuously, exhausting CPU resources?
